info.title: Dataset
info.description: Manage the datasources referenced in Talend Cloud applications and the configuration and metadata associated to these datasources (attributes, data types, data quality).

servers.description: URL for the AWS Europe region
URL for the AWS Asia Pacific region
URL for the AWS United States East region
URL for the AWS Australia region
URL for the Azure United States West region

get.description: A dataset is a collection of data retrieved from a connection. Use this method to list all the datasets that have been created for a given user account.

parameters.name: flat
parameters.description: A dataset is flat when it only contains simple types (not nested)

schema.description: A dataset is flat when it only contains simple types (not nested)

parameters.name: tqlQuery
parameters.description: Filter on dataset's list by using TQL (Talend Query Language)

schema.description: Filter on dataset's list by using TQL (Talend Query Language)

parameters.name: sameSchemaAs
parameters.description: Filter on datasets which have the same schema as the targeted dataset

schema.description: Filter on datasets which have the same schema as the targeted dataset

parameters.name: sortOrder
parameters.description: Order for the sort: ascending or descending, should be paired with the sortBy parameter

schema.description: Order for the sort: ascending or descending, should be paired with the sortBy parameter

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Sort on datasets' list with a field name, should be paired with the sortOrder parameter

schema.description: Sort on datasets' list with a field name, should be paired with the sortOrder parameter

parameters.name: cloudrunner
parameters.description: Filter on dataset's connection remote engine type

schema.description: Filter on dataset's connection remote engine type

get.description: A dataset is a collection of data retrieved from a connection. Use this method to get information on a dataset based on its id.

delete.description: A dataset is a collection of data retrieved from a connection. Use this method to delete a dataset based on its id.

put.summary: Set the certification state of a dataset
put.description:
Use this method to add a certification level to a dataset that has trusted data. There are three certification statuses:
* no certification
* certification pending
* certified

parameters.name: datasetId
parameters.description: Id of dataset to update the certification

schema.description: Id of dataset to update the certification

get.summary: List custom attributes values for a dataset
get.description: A predefined list of values or free text can be used to add metadata to a dataset based on the configured attribute definition. Use this method to list all the custom attributes values that have been applied on a given dataset based on its id.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of rows to ignore before the beginning of the result set

schema.description: The number of rows to ignore before the beginning of the result set

put.summary: Set or update a custom attribute value for a dataset
put.description:
Use this method to set or update a custom attribute value applied on a given dataset.
* In the case of a `List of values` definition, you can select another value among the list of authorized ones.
* In the case of a `Text` definition, you can simply edit the value.

404.description: Custom Attribute or Dataset identifier not found

delete.summary: Delete a custom attribute value for a dataset
delete.description: Use this method to remove a custom attribute value applied on a dataset. When configuring a custom attribute definition, a default value can be set. After removing an existing value, the attribute value of the dataset will be reset to the default one, or to an empty value if no default value was configured beforehand.

404.description: Attribute or Dataset identifier not found

get.description: Custom attributes allow you to create a tagging system for your datasets using free text or predefined values for a given category. Use this method to list all the custom attributes definitions created on the account.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the number of elements returned

schema.description: Limit the number of elements returned

parameters.name: includeMetadata
parameters.description: Add metadata to the response, as the creator full name

schema.description: Add metadata to the response, as the creator full name

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of rows to ignore before the beginning of the result set

schema.description: The number of rows to ignore before the beginning of the result set

post.description: Custom attributes allow you to create a tagging system for your datasets using free text or predefined values for a given category. Use this method to create a new custom attribute definition. The two types of attributes definitions are `List of values` and `Text`.

get.description: Custom attributes allow you to create a tagging system for your datasets using free text or predefined values for a given category. Use this method to retrieve the information on a given custom attribute definition based on its id.

parameters.name: includeMetadata
parameters.description: Add metadata to the response, such as the creator full name

schema.description: Add metadata to the response, such as the creator full name

delete.description: Custom attributes allow you to create a tagging system for your datasets using free text or predefined values for a given category. Use this method to delete a given custom attribute definition based on its id.

patch.description: Custom attributes allow you to create a tagging system for your datasets using free text or predefined values for a given category. Use this method to update the configuration of a given custom attribute definition based on its id.The PATCH operation allows you to apply specific changes to the attribute configuration without replacing it entirely.

get.summary: List metadata of all fields for a dataset

post.summary: Create or update metadata for multiple fields of a dataset

204.description: The metadata has been successfully updated

put.summary: Create or update metadata for a specific field of the dataset

204.description: The metadata has been successfully updated

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path of the field to update the description

schema.description: Path of the field to update the description

description.example: Attribute to configure the business domain of a dataset

mandatory.description: Indicates if this attribute is mandatory

updatedAt.description: Last update timestamp of the attribute

mandatory.description: Indicates if this attribute is mandatory

definitionId.description: The id of the created attribute

mandatory.description: Indicates if this attribute is mandatory

defaults.description: Default values for this attribute/ For the attributes of type 'text', only the first element of the array is stored.

values.description: Values of the attribute, this field is only available for attributes of type 'enum'

entitlements.description: Intersection of TMC user entitlements and the user sharing entitlements

enabled.description: Indicates if the dataset is enabled or not

datastoreId.description: Id of the connection used for the dataset

owner.description: Id of the user who owns the dataset

creator.description: Full name of the user who created the dataset

draft.description: Indicates if the dataset is a draft

favorite.description: Indicates if the dataset is a favorite

trustScoreAxes.description: Trust score details for the dataset

nested.description: Indicates if the dataset is hierarchical

updated.description: Last update timestamp of the dataset

updatedBy.description: User who last updated the dataset

values.description: Current value for the attribute on this dataset

isDefault.description: Indicates if the current value for this dataset matches the default value of the attribute

values.description: Attribute's values to set on the dataset

currentCertification.description: Certification value to set on the dataset

0.description: Number of empty values in the dataset sample

1.description: Number of valid values in the dataset sample

-1.description: Number of invalid values in the dataset sample

total.description: Total number of values in the dataset sample

id.description: ID of the connection used for this dataset

cloudAgentId.description: ID of the engine used by this connection

limit.description: Limit the number of elements returned

offset.description: The number of rows to ignore before the beginning of the result set

contributors.description: Number of users who contributed to this rating

isOwner.description: Indicates if the current user is the owner of the dataset

isSharedWithOthers.description: Indicates if this dataset is shared with other users or groups

description.example: How often a dataset is used

path.description: The path of the field, as returned in the dataset schema

description.description: The description to set for the related path
description.example: This is the full address of the person

ColumnDescriptionUpdateRequest.example: This is the full address of the person

Public.description:
This Bearer authentication can be set in the Authorization header of your requests.

Authentication tokens and Personal Access Tokens are supported.

Personal Access Tokens can be generated in the Profile Preferences page of the Talend Cloud Portal.
